title: Salvia styphelos
intro: Salvia styphelos, also known as Mallee sage or Red-leaf Salvia, is a perennial shrub native to Australia, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The health benefits of Salvia styphelos include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Salvia styphelos is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Salvia styphelos is classified within the family Lamiaceae, which is commonly known as the mint family. This classification places Salvia styphelos alongside other plants such as basil and mint. The genus Salvia includes over 700 species of plants, many of which are native to the Mediterranean region. Salvia styphelos is a unique species within this large and diverse genus. It is further classified as a shrubby perennial herb.
uses: Salvia styphelos has been traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits. Further studies may also reveal potential interactions or side effects when using Salvia styphelos for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Salvia styphelos contains a variety of active constituents, including diterpenoid compounds. These compounds have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The specific diterpenoid compounds found in Salvia styphelos include styphnol and styphnolide.
